evidence_summary: Reviewed the canonical round-222 worktree against master commit 2677b6496d0879a3c14b76c1300206076bd68bff, where HEAD, master, and merge-base all match and the live round exists as an uncommitted worktree diff. The implementation-owned repo-facing scope stays limited to docs/architecture.md, docs/backend-native-pipeline.md, docs/plans/2026-05-02-backend-ir-executable-boundary-mechanism-table.md, src/MLF/Backend/Convert.hs, src/MLF/Backend/IR.hs, src/MLF/Backend/LLVM/Lower.hs, and test/RepoGuardSpec.hs, while orchestrator/state.json, the pointer stubs, the new rev-001 roadmap bundle, and the round-222 lineage files are controller-owned activation bookkeeping. The one-backend-IR contract is synchronized across the planned surfaces, the mechanism-table gate leaves rows 2 through 7 at NO while flipping only row 1 to YES, the focused repo guard passed, and cabal build all && cabal test passed with 2339 examples and 0 failures. The round preserves the inherited explicit-only / non-equi-recursive / non-cyclic-graph / no-fallback predecessor boundary as bounded context only and does not introduce a second public backend IR, lazy STG machinery, cyclic search, multi-SCC search, or roadmap-scope widening.
